title: Create Excalidraw runtime scoring architecture diagram
description:
Implementation task for the runtime scoring architecture diagram.

Scope:
- Inspect existing diagram palette and related architecture diagrams.
- Create a new Excalidraw file in the documentation diagrams area.
- Show Azure-hosted Tethr Kubernetes runtime placement, AWS-hosted GraphQL control plane, and the in-cluster data API for Items, Score Results, and Feedback Items.
- Keep the styling consistent with the existing documentation diagrams.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting diagram work. I inspected the repository diagram standard and confirmed that the new architecture diagram should reuse the shared Excalidraw palette and live under documentation/diagrams. Next step is to inspect existing Azure/AWS architecture diagrams and then draft a new runtime scoring diagram as a separate file so it does not interfere with unrelated in-flight diagram edits.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Visual direction chosen from the existing diagram set: pale cloud-boundary containers, gray subsystem containers, white service cards, and direct labeled arrows. Planned content is an AWS control-plane area on the left, an Azure/Tethr Kubernetes runtime area on the right, and explicit runtime arrows from the Plexus scoring pod to the AWS GraphQL control plane and the in-cluster data API.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Drafted the new Excalidraw asset at documentation/diagrams/Runtime Scoring Architecture.excalidraw. The diagram uses the existing repository styling and explicitly separates the AWS control plane from the Azure/Tethr runtime plane, with labeled runtime edges from the scoring pod to the AWS GraphQL API and to the in-cluster data API for Items, Score Results, and Feedback Items.
